Handcrafted complex rhythms accompanied by heart-stained pads and overwhelming space is a weakness of mine. Although being underrated in today's time equates to the lack of followers, I think the handful that do take time to listen to Intracktion's music always experience something intellectually, sonically and aesthetically enlightening.

I have built up a small arsenal of techniques that really define my sound but I’m always adding to it as I’m never completely satisfied. about:
Name: Kai Franke
Place: Hilden (Germany)
Age: 40 
Profession: Musician (self thought)

interview.a: He always had problems to define music.

There was a piano in the family home. His mother remembered how as a small boy he would clamber onto the piano stool, listening to every note with his full concentration. After some months he was playing little tunes with one hand, some months later extemporizing with with two. At the age of eight he started taking piano lessons, but found it boring to learn to play notes. He preferred playing things that came out of himself, not from a paper full of notes. When he was 14 his father bought him a Korg T1. He loved this big keyboard. It had a lot of great synths and drums and a sequencer, so for the first time he could record his compositions on discs. He becam interested in forms of synthesis, creating his own sounds with a Yamaha SY99. With the development of personal computers, recording tools and plug-in technology he did more and more with the computer down to the present time.
At the age of three or four
About two years ago
„Let´s make some music!“

about:
Name: Nicklas Sindlev Anderse
Place: Copenhagen, Denmark. Living in Odense at the moment.
Age: 21
Profession: Student at University of Southern Denmark, studying applied mathematics.

interview.a: Clean melodic Chill-Out

Nicklas took piano lessons at a jazz musician for about 6 years without learning the theory. It was more about playing around and using music as a creative tool to express himself. He learned to play different instruments, but primarily the piano, which he learned to play by ear.
He started creating his own music when he joined Audiotool. Before that he had played cover songs on the piano.
2011 via Google appstore. Attracted by RNZR's remix of "When stars collide" by Hbeao.

For me, the most important part of making music, is having fun. Audiotool makes this effortless with its intuitive and straightforward layout. With the amazing sample browser at hand and a few simple effects, its easy to make my creativity to flow. Without Audiotool I would probably never have started producing any music or even continued to play an instrument (sorry if this sounds like a bad commercial, but I mean it). For the community i just want to say: Make the music you want to make, don't make the music everyone else wants you to make. Good artists evolve, when they follow their heart, think outside the box and experiment. New sounds and concepts can evolve from simple ideas and this is what makes music such an amazing thing.

2009 on the Tony-B Machine, when someone posted a link to Hobnox Audiotool. He didn't know how to use the devices, so he put it aside until 2010 when he created an account. Cripta happened to be a user on the Tony-B Machine so he was a welcome presence on Audiotool

Digital audio workstations (DAWs) exist to assist in the making of music. But that doesn't mean they are easy ways to escape hard work. A DAW is like any other instrument; you must practice in order to gain experience and get better. If you find that your work isn't up to your standards, then familiarity with your tools is more important than more tools. After all, you don't know what that knob does until you twist it, so you should twist it before you get more knobs. So my advice for Audiotoolers is this: if you want to make better music, then eat more peaches.

tracks.desc: I have this as my ringtone :) I opened this up had a look and learned tons, so simple and yet the sound design is so good, the beats, those little blips, that base and that title..... totally weird, totally brilliant.

tracks.name: A Certain Kind of Bullet
tracks.desc: And now we're getting into the 303 side of things! This is the kind of music I love in clubs; hard, melodic and with a gritty acid line going through it. Check out the 303 work at 02:42, then the drop at 03:30 I'd be going nuts at that bit!

tracks.name: The Simpleton (2014)
tracks.desc: A seriously talented dude who was a massive help early on in AT, he even took the time to make me a template explaining compression, truely

tracks.name: Jalousie
tracks.desc: I wish I could make music like this, my god he's good at doing glitched up percs and beats! And when the vocals come in..... yeah. This guy deserves way more faves than he gets, I literally have an album of his stuff on itunes that'll take me a day to listen to, get on his page and get listening!

Again if I'm making a list Inavon has got to be in it. In my opinion he's the best at making trance on AT, his arps and pads are always spot on and then mixing in the 303 - perfect, I'd be very happy bouncing along to this on a beach in Goa!

tracks.name: "Another life" Vocals by KrazieKammie (rec in AT)
Fresh Beatz
tracks.desc: As soon as I heard this I opened it up to study how Infy made this

tracks.name: Bass Up
tracks.desc: This is definitely one of my all time AT favorites. Everyday, I strive to make EDM Trap tracks as epic as this one as well as some other tracks on Aodiotool. The drop rendered me speechless. I don't think I could easily get sick of listening to this.

tracks.name: Solaris (feat. Luxior)
tracks.desc: What I love about this song is how chill yet powerful it sounds. The chord progression and melody were excellent. The work that was put into this track definitely shows.

tracks.name: Time Is Candy (with Ignapon3, Luxior, Almate, Teqtoniq and Kavil)
tracks.desc: When I saw the title of this track, I knew it was going to be great; it wasn't what I expected when I first played it, but I fell in love with it. Needless to say, the track was very well produced, but my favorite part, the part that kills me is the drop tag; love it!

tracks.name: Sole Survivor
tracks.desc: The dark atmosphere of the track in the intro just keeps you suspended and waiting to see what happens. I loved the live-sounding drums, the chord pregression, and how the track was arranged; it just moved me.

tracks.name: rabbit
tracks.desc: The serenity of this track captured my attention. I'm a big fan of piano tracks and calm/chill tracks so this one was the best of both worlds for me.

tracks.name: three minutes after the world died
Under The Same Sun
tracks.desc: Olaf is the first artist whose atmospheric music touched me. I have always followed him and paid attention to his creations which are always of very good taste.

tracks.name: Planes
tracks.desc: Julsy lies halfway between the very quiet music and the faster, more rhythmic tracks. But it's always in a Zen spirit which I like so much.

tracks.name: Stellar magnetic field
tracks.desc: It's Daftwill's frank to and fro between rhythmic and

tracks.name: Emancipation
tracks.desc: With Cihangir I glide with delight in the sensual and hot music into the depth of night. His music has always appeared exceptionally pure, both in terms of the spirit of its sounds, its arrangements ... well, for me it is a must.

tracks.name: autoscontro
tracks.desc: Music composed by Cripta will serve me to build a bridge to the next track of this album. His style is also very wide-ranging, this is what I like. He always maintains an excellent musical taste, which in my opinion places him among the best sound designers of Audiotool. For me, this is a must-musician.

tracks.name: Rise
tracks.desc: So Jambosh is the one who I have always felt close to. I mean the style, sound etc. I've always loved the high quality of his tracks but also and above all the energy that is always there. This boy has always fired Audiotool with positive vibrations. Another recommendation.

tracks.name: Surface
tracks.desc: Jake Tyler OH MY GOSH!!! His music is always amazing. It's full of feeling and emotion which is always key when producing music. This song specifically I listen to when I am angry or like when I need to get something done. He makes the most beautiful music, filled with passion which is clearly seen in all his work. Every song he makes just keeps getting better and better.

tracks.name: The Knot at the Rope's End
tracks.desc: I mean come on, it's Inavon. I like reflecting my styles off of his in general but this song is one of my all time favorites by him. This song reminded me of the 80’s trance and I love that era of music. Me and my Dad listen to this a bunch when we drive.

tracks.name: Dirty Resurrection
tracks.desc: French synths, equal to angle voices. Quite Lovely. This group only has two songs but they are awesome and to me they are easy to listen to and definately give off that french like tone.

tracks.name: Stare at the Sun
tracks.desc: Uprising is amazing and has always been so supportive and nice to me. He is a great person and an amazing musician. When I first started audiotool, I thought he was the coolest person and wanted to be as known as he was. This song is cool and full of layers which I love incorporating in my music.

tracks.name: At The End
tracks.desc: This influenced me through it’s genre. I love the presets and sound incorporated into this song. EIDØ LON makes awesome stuff and I wish they were still creating works because they are fantastic, Everything blends perfectly and always makes my day.

tracks.name: It's My Time To Shine (dedicated to 21st Century)
Control
tracks.desc: The pads and the pure innocence of this track and of all Gravidon’s music makes me so happy. There is not one single track by them that I have ever disapproved of. I enjoy the mix of feelings provoked by his music.

tracks.name: Testees (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: Awesome track - Astrum - what a talented Audiotool artist ! Stunning .

tracks.name: Winter sky
tracks.desc: Beautiful music by sbronz. I close my eyes, hear your music, smell the wind and feel the sun … like at home. This music leaves me with a sense of empowerment : I miss sbronz.

tracks.name: Papa was an Audiotooler he had the Blues
tracks.desc: Funk, Jazz and Audiotool – That's South Bronx Den . His tracks are unique, every one of them. And all are recognizable as his very special personal style.

tracks.name: My Industrial
tracks.desc: Intense industrial track by White Grizzly. Fantastic and deep are his sounds . What a trip in White Grizzly's world.

tracks.name: summer base 1
tracks.desc: Japan + music + gaming = pot … He deserves much, much more support . Great artist, very talented and so underrated … shame on you!

tracks.name: minutes of your time
tracks.desc: audioarchitekt : „ lay down, put on your headphones, turn up to a suiting volume and let me take a few minutes of your time... „ I do

tracks.name: amaterasu / wren
tracks.desc: It's time, soul and music , own samples and Audiotool … emotional tracks by dronealpha.

tracks.name: tijuana
tracks.desc: Excellent Chill track with great sounds of the southern- vibe of Tijuana .

tracks.name: Delete You
tracks.desc: Another collab track with a good melody. Through this, Astrum and Reversal showed me that hard-hitting tracks can be made on Audiotool. This helped me realize the music style I love the most, too!

tracks.name: Drawing In A Bar
tracks.desc: Frigolito is just insanely talented! His work leaves me in awe all the time. This particular track by him is one of my favorites because as fast paced as it is, it taught me that Audiotool isn't limited to sounds that sound like they are electronically made.

tracks.name: Jam and Toast
tracks.desc: Wow if you're feeling Saturday morning fun, this is a great track! I love the melodies in it and the blend of styles in it. You can also hear some

tracks.name: Cadmium Hotel
tracks.desc: Definitely my favorite Techno on this site. It blends the Neurofunk styles of the wonderful CGman and the underrated technohead KaTaNa. The bass is so great!

tracks.name: Make Me Feel
tracks.desc: Phenom is so underrated that a colony of ants can go over his tracks with ease. I fell in love with this track when I first listened to it mainly because it's my favorite style of Nu-Disco. This track taught me that sampling on Audiotool can be a very beautiful thing.

tracks.name: Guiding Moonlight
tracks.desc: Inavon was my greatest trance inspiration. This track has two things that I absolutely LOVE to hear in Audiotool tracks: piano and formant filter choir pads. Choir pads!! Truly a gem.

tracks.name: Iwi Biwi Back
tracks.desc: Amazing depth and superb vocals!

tracks.name: Palladium Feast
tracks.desc: Nothing can beat the bass in this! @CGMan and @flying-baby-seal made some bad-ass head-banging tunes!

tracks.name: Off to Tokyo
tracks.desc: VAPEROUS just does things. And they're done well. Now with this new name, I think VAPEROUS is moving onto a new direction. So exciting!

tracks.name: 17nm
tracks.desc: Noraus 's sound design is irrefutably amazing. I can't say more! Hope he'll do more music :)

tracks.name: Megaman 3 ( Bitstepper Mix )
tracks.desc: One of the first tracks I listened to on Audiotool! I also found out about @beat123 (Luxior) through this track. @infyuthsion did an amazing remix on this one.

tracks.name: Office Bossa Nova
tracks.desc: I really like Frigolito's music. Lots of Jazz elements. This one is Bossa Nova - very relaxing!

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.desc: This is one of the best Hip Hop tracks on Audiotool! Always keeps me grooving.

tracks.name: Tagadà
tracks.desc: Cripta doesn't always make Synthpop, but when he does, it blows my mind!

tracks.name: Requiem
tracks.desc: I have been following Boyles since he was Nowai. Although he delivers awesomeness on multiple genres, I really love his House Tracks. This Deep House piece is just one of many inspiring tracks in his discothequé! Chill yet vibrant...

tracks.name: 7 chords
tracks.desc: My favorite track from the Italian Techno Beast! Chords, Bassline, and that awesome melodic twist after the break. Loving everything about it.

tracks.name: Ibiza Club (Electric Love)
tracks.desc: One of those amazing deep house tracks you listen to when chilling out after six hours of dancefloor marathon and bam! you feel recharged all of a sudden and start dancing again. Amazing Track!!

tracks.name: Technicolour
tracks.desc: I love Akimbo's Style! I wasn't familiar with the genre but Akimbo made me love liquid after I discovered this masterpiece. Banger!

tracks.name: Heis-T'!:)
tracks.desc: One of those tracks I keep coming back for. Over and over. Still I'm not sure why, lol, I mean Great bass and beat, amazing smooth atmosphere but probably just because of its fresh sound, which is above genres. Uber groovy, great dance track! 10. Steal You Away by @trance10

tracks.name: blend
tracks.desc: I dunno if kavil puts emotion into his songs. But it sure does sound like it. His stuff really relaxes me.

tracks.name: Night Run
tracks.desc: Long live luxior.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2014 - opaqity
tracks.desc: Opaqity is a genius with this track. I always try to make a super cool string synth, and this one is just executed perfectly. Best string synth on AT imo.

tracks.name: East Westwood
tracks.desc: Xavrockbeats is so badass. I can't fathom how beautiful this man is.

tracks.name: There once was
tracks.desc: I love how weird TopHat is.

tracks.name: Cerulean
tracks.desc: Second best song on AT if you ask me. I love ... and his style of keeping things obscurity. He's also great when it comes to manipulating samples and things like that.

tracks.name: Bounce
tracks.desc: Reminds me of my ex-ex-ex-girlfriend. Oh sorry. The track.. Great stuff Aaron, thanks for this piece of art. And I still listen to this.

tracks.name: EVERYDAY ANYONE
tracks.desc: This one is one of my favorites. Unique. Great dark atmosphere. By the way, I have started a remix of this, but I haven't finished because of job/university. I Hope I'll finish it some day.

tracks.name: The Core
tracks.desc: I know him as Plus+. One of the greatest Drum and Bass artists on Audiotool. This track is one of my favorites of his. Love the Reggae vibes, drums and everything else.

tracks.name: Ubongo
tracks.desc: Don't forget about CGMAN. The greatest Neurofunk artist on Audiotool. Thanks for this masterpiece. Basses are killing. Drums are perfect and unique.

tracks.name: Spin'Ed
tracks.desc: Perfect use of vocals and all the glitch stuff and of course the drums.

tracks.name: Reckless
tracks.desc: It is almost kind of sad that I discovered Olaf and his superior Garage music only 2-3 months ago but ever since I´m in love! You can clearly see that Olaf is a huge inspiration for me now as I tried to create a futuristic garage Beat in my Track

tracks.name: Unrealize
tracks.desc: EVERYBODY!! I found Burials secret AT account! There is no other track here on Audiotool that is so well produced in every way. There isn´t even any Future Garage track close to the quality of this one. Prove me wrong. Pro tip: You can't.

tracks.name: young
tracks.desc: My personal favorite by the great amoeba. you may disagree with my choice because there are much more popular tracks by him but the fact that

tracks.name: Midnight
tracks.desc: I could have taken every VLTRA song ever. He never disappoints me in any way. His music is definitely the most artistic on Audiotool. Props.

tracks.name: Jonjon - Breach of Trust (Akimbo and Jambams future Jungle Remix)
tracks.desc: Another friend of mine is Akimbo. Those of you who have known me a little longer might have noticed that I changed my style completely over the years. Back in the days of

tracks.name: Plane
tracks.desc: I hope you get famous one day boys. Best group on AT. Best song in this list.

tracks.name: Black
tracks.desc: Fantastic sounds, and I love the progressions, especially in the first third.

tracks.name: Infernal loop
tracks.desc: Great sounds on this track. It’s long one and a simple arrangement, but I think it really holds the attention.

tracks.name: Die Ruhe nach dem Sturm
tracks.desc: Amazingly accomplished, in terms of mood, this really does evoke acts like Harmonia for me – early to mid-70s German electronic.

tracks.name: mega device multiplied amplified
tracks.desc: Really cool one. Simple but really driving. I love the droning bass.

tracks.name: Blood Runs Cold
tracks.desc: This is a fairly recent track. It’s an example of combining physical analogue synths with Audiotool’s effects and sequencing – with the most overtly analogue sound of all – the bassline – being supplemented by the Pulverisateur.

tracks.name: The stars dance for me (sketch)
tracks.desc: Really nice, I love how it sorta builds up and then goes back down and then the climax hits you.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: This one is outstanding first just because of the synthesis. Try opening it up, it's just crazy how he got those sounds. I wish it ended nicer but everything else is perfect.

tracks.name: woes
tracks.desc: I really wish enjoyyourshoes would keep making stuff like this, very beautiful and unique.

tracks.name: dapper
tracks.desc: I couldn't find my favorite track by him cuz he has like 500 so I just put this. It's still perfect, it's cool how he’s able to make so much awesome music while still using so much repetition.

tracks.name: Northern comfort
tracks.desc: This one is just awesome, it's repetitive but keeps the listener engaged. And I especially love the sound design and the detail of it. It's really unique and sorta lo-fi. and the movement of it all is great too.

tracks.name: We Fall
tracks.desc: Very beautiful track and awesome use of vocal samples.

tracks.name: Tides
tracks.desc: This track I really love. Just amazing.

tracks.name: ノスタルジア
tracks.desc: I love this, it's so tranquil but what's cool is that the sample is actually Clair de Lune and he was able to transform it into something completely different and equally beautiful.

tracks.name: high places
tracks.desc: At first I didn't like this, sounded too pop-like/EDM-ish but actually it's grown on me. The percussion could be fixed a bit but other than that I like it. It's pretty accessible and sounds pretty good too.

tracks.name: Airglow
tracks.desc: Kepz is able to achieve a depth with his music that most aren't capable of. This holds true with Airglow, a dynamically lush and airy journey from beginning to end that takes you out of the chair in which you're probably sitting. I hope to meet this guy some day.

tracks.name: Family
tracks.desc: This is about as good as it gets when it comes to collaborations on Audiotool. Jace (J. Fields) supplies a flowering backdrop of pads and light percussion samples while Julsy saturates the scene with her gorgeous drifting voice and thoughtful lyrics.

tracks.name: Line ∞
tracks.desc: Perspective displays his wonderful composition abilities in this one. The two-step styled drums fit perfectly with the melody and crushed bass elements that weave in and out of the patterns. This one's gentle, but it's bound to get stuck in your head.

tracks.name: Spite
tracks.desc: The feeling this song invokes is so unique; a blend of nostalgia and anguish. It also makes you question the timing and tempo as it switches through drum grooves of differing paces. The last resolve when the original beat kicks back in with an additional lower harmony hits home every time.

tracks.name: AFTERGLOW ((Jonjon Mix) + deep1tone)
tracks.desc: While we're on the subject of uplifting Drum and Bass, this track will really have you flying. 3 great minds put energy into this thing and its clear. The whole Afterglow remix contest brought about some pretty awesome creations.

tracks.name: teeth.
tracks.desc: I had to have rnzr on this list. This guy... not only is he great fun to be around, he has created some of the most monstrous combinations of tools and cables the audiotool app has ever seen, with a sound to match. This track was absolutely jaw dropping when I first heard it, and still is. rnzr. creates music with an extremely high level of mindfulness and creativity.

tracks.name: Cheerful tunes for the end of summer
tracks.desc: There are so many friends and artists that I love from Audiotool that deserve a spot on this list. As I was browsing my

tracks.name: The Hope
tracks.desc: When I think of White Grizzly I picture a man living deep in a eternally snow filled mountain range, a man who snowshoes forty miles uphill just to find an internet signal with which to publish his apocalyptically beautiful soundscapes. I was going to pick a different track by him initially, but then he published this one the other day and it took the honors!

tracks.name: Low Pressure
tracks.desc: Syntax was one of the first artists I got into on AT. The way she writes her music just really makes a lot of sense to me. I basically learned how to use the pulv by opening her tracks and studying them, not to mention sidechaining, velocity and a bunch of other stuff I use on a regular basis. Low pressure is still one of my favorites by her. I love everything about it. The bass, the percs, and that clean clean clean Syntax sound!

tracks.name: Untitled
tracks.desc: Mikke is a genius. His sound design is some of the best and most inspiring I've heard on AT. Not to mention, he's an incredibly nice and humble guy. This track says more than I ever could about the brilliance of his music.

tracks.name: ground control
tracks.desc: Speaking of incredibly nice guys.... Tornsage: Broken Philosopher, selfless promoter of other artists, and eternal supporter of new ATers. One never knows exactly what to expect from a Tornsage track, but you always know it's going to be GREAT!!! Thanks for all of the support, Torn! :)

tracks.name: Allure
tracks.desc: The definition of a lazy summer afternoon. The perfect kickback song when it’s 3pm on a Sunday. Just you, the homies, some beer, and the sun shining down. That is Allure.

tracks.name: ｗｈｙ ｄｉｄ ｔｈｅ ｌｉｇｈｔ ｆａｄｅ
tracks.desc: Raw Emotion. Raw, Schizophrenic Emotion. It takes a mad scientist, to construct the story this song tells. Ignore the technical marvels, and lose yourself to the emotion this song engulfs you with.

tracks.name: the last thing
tracks.desc: Witch House is a unique thing, and there are very few that can understand and showcase it’s brilliance. Nominal is Witch House in my opinion. The Last Thing sets an amazing tone and ambience that can’t be replicated by any other artist. The infusion of different styles really sell this as one of his best tracks.

tracks.name: [Phresh.]
tracks.desc: I’m fairly certain OBN is signed. This track is one of the many reasons why he is signed. That theme song you have when you walk down the street, and you know everybody gives a crap about you? This is it. On repeat for the 344th time.

Experimentation. Experimentation drives innovation. Devoid ¾ was my first touch into Future Garage, and my first exposure to the legend we all know as VLTRA. Plus, Natalie Portman :p and the original is just as epic.

tracks.name: tranquility
tracks.desc: Don't let the title fool you; this is one of the most hardcore and eclectic neuro-hop tracks I've ever heard. The first drop is reminiscent of Billain's style, while the second drop is head-bobbingly funky with extremely well-honed syncopation. Luxior did an outstanding job here, and think the joke element to the track is great as well :P

tracks.name: Nerd
tracks.desc: A nice little fun jazzy dnb piece reminds me of a spy chase scene of some kind. However, the production quality is sublime, with unique and sophisticated chords and synthwork. Hats off to Gno4166.

tracks.name: wait
tracks.desc: This is a track that invokes pure and raw emotion. The pad and bass combination is really just outstanding. The pitched vocals in the background make for some great subtle touches and help complete the bittersweet and sad vibe. Overall a prime example of the amazing future garage that Audiotool has to offer.

tracks.name: vitamine DnB
tracks.desc: THRONE (or PLUS+, the name that I originally knew him by) has crafted some of the most professional-sounding liquid dnb on Audiotool, this track included. The moving bassline is so creative, and the typical PLUS+ plucks are meaty as ever. Oh, and don't forget that amazingly atmospheric vibe in the intro and the airy leads. And that dang swing halftime. The fact that this track was fairly overlooked compared to other amazing liquid tracks by him makes no sense to me.

tracks.name: Firefly (Ft. Luxior)
tracks.desc: Yo, so I liked this chord progression so much that I learned to play it on piano. The suspension at the end of the progression is just so dang tasteful. Oh yeah, and the chord chops are absolutely nuts. Overall, an amazing fast paced banger that has been on replay for me since 7 months ago.

tracks.name: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe
tracks.desc: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe by enjoyyourshoes is a very successful experimental track

tracks.name: Papa is an Audiotooler
tracks.desc: Papa is an Audiotooler (remix) by Fontana showcases why he is known as the

tracks.name: escape
tracks.desc: Escape by Invisible Green Spider aka DroneAlpha combines thought provoking music and takes the listener to a place of his soul he didn't know he had

tracks.name: Summit
tracks.desc: Summit by Mad-Scientist-Mike takes you to the top, and the bass work keeps your feet rooted firmly to the dance floor

tracks.name: Can't Get Enough of Your Love
Alive Comeback
tracks.desc: I really don't have a favorite track of my own but I remember a track I did back when Hobnox was around called

tracks.name: In A Trance(the day my son was born)
When Stars Collide
tracks.desc: RNZR’s best in my opinion

tracks.name: Shut up and Fuck
tracks.desc: Elia Trix – a masterful producer who I wish would come back and continue.

tracks.name: The Snow
tracks.desc: I had to include the beautiful work of vile – always an amazing mix and a great influence.

tracks.name: please
tracks.desc: Amoeba – what more can one say than what has already been said – a true talent and a beautiful piece of music.

tracks.name: The Hunt Is On ( Hunter X Hunter Bump )
tracks.desc: I included this because I love everything offbeat does – this was an especially influential bump probably because it was some of the first work I heard from him. One of the best artists on audiotool.

tracks.name: The sound of the time
tracks.desc: The grizz in a deeply enigmatic artist who’s music has no parallel and who has influenced me with everything he produces.

tracks.name: Monoscope
tracks.desc: This is a track I collaborated with invisible green spider on and to this day remains one of my favs – I think it has a lot to do with the story or the idea behind the track.

tracks.name: ravenshead
tracks.desc: > <script type=

tracks.name: bobby
tracks.desc: I have been hooked to this track since I listened to it for the first time. This one is like reading a book, it tells a story. And has some of the best bassline, leads, pads and percussions I've ever heard.

tracks.name: KTN-34
tracks.desc: I'd have liked to include all of KaTaNa production in this edition, but I'm only limited to ten :) His tracks are just technically perfect and his sounds breathtaking. I learned so much by just listening to his tracks, let alone opening them! He helped me a lot in understanding why and how to use filters, EQs, etc. Thanks man!

tracks.name: techno breakfast
tracks.desc: Christian-Chrom is the bomb! You can feel the energy punching you in the face while his tracks are playing. Combine this with great technical skills and productivity (he got currently 82 tracks in less than a year!) and you got the basic Christian-Chrom.

tracks.name: Synthetik
tracks.desc: in5omniac is another AT Techno hero. His clean and powerful sounds build up a gritty minimal Techno whose rhythm will shake your bones. This one is just a great example of how he's going to become a famous producer ;)

tracks.name: Vellicator
tracks.desc: I love how Alan Ross took some noises and arranged them in such a beauty! I think this track was made to introduce the Rasselbock if I remember correctly.

tracks.name: Nor
tracks.desc: This track is craaaaaazy good. The amount of detail put into the background is astonishing. Not to mention the bass is incredibly good, one of my favorite collaborations on Audiotool.

tracks.name: The Final Scene
tracks.desc: I've loved this track ever since the day it came out. I listen to this track so much you'd think it'd be the most annoying thing on earth to me, but no. I can't ever get enough of this track, and xav never fails to impress.

tracks.name: Last Chance
tracks.desc: One of the most professionally produced songs on Audiotool. The setup, organization, sound design, chords, melodies, etc.. Everything about this just does it for me, really great.

tracks.name: Most Popular Song Of The Hour
tracks.desc: This track is sooooo goooooood. The melody during the drop gets me literally every time I listen. Crazy professional.

tracks.name: Anna (Notoz Remix)
tracks.desc: One of the most beautiful tracks I've heard on audiotool. Great spin on the original.

tracks.name: Airwaves
tracks.desc: Whoa! this track is so powerful and pushing! This is the soundtrack I would choose if I had to run from helicopters shooting with laser beams. I think my heartbeat would have had a dropout on the break at 1:55.

tracks.name: Airwaves (Teqtoniq Remix)
tracks.desc: Wow...this is just brutal! Loved the original and this gave me the rest. Love the buildup so much and then the drop with the vocals ...crazy… No, seriously this is epic, very clean synths and a great mixing quality.

tracks.name: Highway Cruisin'
tracks.desc: This track could be directly out of my Spotify track list. Melodically and funky Synth Pop with an overwhelming professionalism. I`m in love with the synth design and the clever alternations in the melody.

tracks.name: Coming Back To You
tracks.desc: Jordi Moragues ...what an impressive artist! I would have preferred to list all of his tracks here. The emotionality in his work and his talent for playing with the expectations of the audience is unique. I chose this track due to the beautiful fusion of perfect Audiotool synth work and this wonderful violin record.

tracks.name: white lies
tracks.desc: It is absolutely mind-blowing how genius Luxior works with his groove and various puzzle pieces in his tracks. Every second you hear an unexpected noise that fits perfectly into the shuffled rhythm structure. Outstanding mix and synth work combined with lots of talent and passion.

tracks.name: Ghetto Blaster
tracks.desc: Retro music at its best! Wonderful job with the synths and percussion in this track. I love this retro Synth Wave style and he captured it with perfection.

tracks.name: Bottleneck
tracks.desc: I could listen to this track for hours, it has a great groove and some of the cleanest and best-mixed synths on Audiotool. The creative note pattern never loses the attention of the listener and brings new elements throughout .

tracks.name: why did i fade away
tracks.desc: This song has everything. I love the dreamy atmosphere and the on-point percussion. The synth design and the composition blows me away every time I listen to this piece of art. Opaqity has the talent to create something that is more than the sum of its parts.

tracks.name: solace
tracks.desc: Solace by Tornsage is one of those tracks that make you forget time and the environment around you. Every note is a step more in this emotional dream world he is creating. It’s like reading a book of emotions. Sad and full of hope at the same time.

tracks.name: trip 17
tracks.desc: Each time RNZR publishes a track, he makes everybody agree: each of his pieces is awesome, this one particularly for me ... Very spacey sounds, with only a kick as percussion, it transports you far above the clouds ... I remember I listened to it in a loop during a plane trip while watching through the window ... Mind blowing!

tracks.name: Train Of Fear
tracks.desc: For me Frigolito is the best composer on Audiotool. The man who can make synths sing! Damn, that track ... I can't explain it, it's simply a drop of acid in your ears, nothing more ;)

tracks.name: Karma Ex
tracks.desc: For a long time my favorite by him was

tracks.name: FUKT
tracks.desc: Together with Jonjon, the best DnB producer on Audiotool. This track is so bluffing! It expresses so much violence. I feel so much blackness when listening to it. A real horror movie soundtrack ! And damn ... look at that picture!

tracks.name: Broken Projector
tracks.desc: This track ... I have spent so much time, listening to it while watching sunsets, stars, landscapes, just thinking ... So brought with it so many emotions ... and this guitar synth. What a beauty! I love the kind of Trance Kepz produces! I always have good times while listening to his stuff.

tracks.name: Shifted
tracks.desc: Oh man ... what a bomb! CGMan made me discover the Neurofunk with his banging tracks! How he makes his synth roar on this track ... Unbelievable. Each track of his is pure genius! 9. The Nightmare by @soundgeek aka 808chunk

tracks.name: Fluid
tracks.desc: A huge track too! Another Audiotool genius! I love Ascent and his banging Techno tracks! This one has an extrem darkness! And its sound design, is for me one of the best on Audiotool.

tracks.name: little devil
tracks.desc: My favorite Techno track on Audiotool! This is simply perfection, nothing more to say ... You could play it in loop during a party all night long without a problem!

tracks.name: an ending
tracks.desc: I love to mess around with the oldies from some of the greatest artists on Audiotool ... this is what I found one year ago. And if he says that he always ends up on this one, then yeah it's good enough :)

tracks.name: 200th
tracks.desc: Ok ladies and gentlemen 200 + and still counting ... Thanks for your support and critics on my weak basses :) We are waiting for your 228th ...

tracks.name: schnibbel it
tracks.desc: Talking about Techno? Olondro is the real guy ... He always brings fantastic powerful sound. I still remember when he faved my track for the first time, I knew I was doing the right thing :)

tracks.name: GRIK6:)!
tracks.desc: Who can stay indifferent to this beat? Right now while I'm writing, I'm dancing on the grik6. Fantastic groove, probably my all time favorite.

tracks.name: Preacher man
tracks.desc: Ok this is getting serious :) Great collaboration, so much power, just great.

tracks.name: That's What They Want You to Think
tracks.desc: When I met The Seahorse he was doing some big beat stuff and I liked it. In the meantime he has developed his previous style with some new influences and now he is one of the greatest artists on Audiotool. Keep working brother ! 10. Kill the Noise ... by @brainwalker

tracks.name: steampunked ted
tracks.desc: I already said it in the comments: I have great feelings when I hear it. Dabrig is a master of little details, everyone should listen to him more often. Do it now!

tracks.name: Living With Stones
tracks.desc: Never forget this track!

tracks.name: Rotom
tracks.desc: Again too few favs. It's a good Breakbeat track with a nice second part. Listen to his other tracks as well! Artist recommendation: @chroller

tracks.name: Carsberg
tracks.desc: All time favorite of mine. Techno94 created amazing tracks when Audiotool was still very young and there was no Heisenberg. You should check out all the tracks on his profile. It’s a shame he is no longer active. If someone knows him: please make him come back!

tracks.name: Recluse
tracks.desc: This was the hardest to pick for me since Olaf has released so many great tracks. I think Recluse is an absolute Audiotool masterpiece. It is one of those songs I listen to while working late at night.

tracks.name: nightlight
tracks.desc: Opaqity is my personal favorite Audiotool user actively releasing tracks. I love his music with all the subtle details. Also great to listen to while walking to work, working and walking from work back home.

tracks.name: Guitar
tracks.desc: One of those early classics which used the - at that time brand-new - sample player. Just recently I listened to Baba by Mux Mool again. Quite funny when you compare it to Yas’ track from a year before. Maybe I missed something in 2011/2012 but it shows once more that you don’t need expensive equipment to create great tracks.

tracks.name: Spite
tracks.desc: I can’t decide which one to pick. There is Spite and there is Submerge. Both very different tracks and beautiful in their own way. Spite is definitely more whorled or “quirlig” as we Germans say.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: The synthesizer connoisseur at his best. We’re all grateful for the fantastic Heisenberg presets created by Noraus. I had a bunch of songs on shuffle and this one started to play while I was lying on the beach, enjoying a cold beverage and reading about purely functional data structures. My personal happy place.

tracks.name: Machine Autopsy
tracks.desc: I want to end this list with a lovely experimental track. I also want to encourage you to try to create your own unique sounds and to continue to push the limits of Audiotool!

tracks.name: Reaching
tracks.desc: When I discovered this song I thought that I was in a sea of calm. I love every voice, every single synth that make the melody. My mind escapes with this.

tracks.name: Frigid.
tracks.desc: As you can tell I really love the chill stuff because it means everything to me and I feel it so deeply. Also I can love Trap/Vibe/Orgasmic/Chill and this is where Lewi comes in: beautiful melodies with some trap beats. It’s a blessing.

tracks.name: You Found Me
tracks.desc: It’s the perfect piece mixing styles. I found it pretty chill and pretty electronic and when the beat comes in I dance alone. I wish that more music like this could be created.

tracks.name: About time
tracks.desc: > <script type=

tracks.name: Impact
tracks.desc: I will always know him as plus + . No matter his name this is another liquid Drum and Bass producer I’ll gladly include in my list. This track was another eye-opener for me. The first time I heard a Reese-like sub in an Audiotool track. I studied the crap out of it. Yet could never really copy it  another amazing talent.

tracks.name: ...and then all this crazy-ass robots appeared
R
tracks.desc: Surely Oedipax had to be on the list. She always makes the darkest Drum and Bass. This one in particular has the typical Oedipax signature. The layered drums. The eerie background sounds. The Trance-like synths. A massive uplifting break. She truly is the queen of Drum and Bass. May her reign be long lasting.

tracks.name: Limitless
tracks.desc: The Amen breaks, the synths, the pacing … the energy of this track ... every time I listen to it I get goose bumps. This really was another one of those tracks that blew me away. Probably not the best-known track from Notoz but to me it is his best by far. Probably because in all the uplifting, glitched Amen drums there’s this growling dark bass telling me to run for my life ;)

tracks.name: A Completely Normal Day
tracks.desc: The guy is crazy we all know that. What better track to showcase it with. Jambam has a great talent for combining sounds and find melodies in the chaos he creates. His glitch work on this track is probably the best on Audiotool. And his experiments are always very inspirational to me. One of the truly great minds on AT.

tracks.name: system of a system
tracks.desc: I love the concept of this track. AudioArchitect is one of the best artists I have come across when it comes to articulately expressing his state of mind through his music.

tracks.name: evolutionary humanism
tracks.desc: This track has some wonderful old school elements in it. The bass in this track is hypnotic to me. Whenever I hear this track, it generally takes about 24 hours to completely work its way through my brain.

tracks.name: Auf Kurs (2015)
tracks.desc: A recent remake by this prolific and legendary AT artist, this track showcases signature Sndbrgn sound that can be found in his work, regardless of genre; intricate percussion with mysterious synth work delicately layered on top.

tracks.name: Waiting Door
tracks.desc: Another AT legend typically known for dark Techno tracks, this track shows that Cripta is a versatile artist with outstanding synthpop sensibilities (which can also be found in the best collaboration on AT – @sandcripten ).

tracks.name: leck am reaktor
tracks.desc: The synth work in this track still haunts me (in a good way) to this day. Where the heck did this guy go?!

tracks.name: invaders
tracks.desc: Christian-Chrom is a more recent artist I stumbled upon. I wish I had heard his tracks earlier. He is another who likes to have fun and experiment with different synths and tools much like noraus. Another great inspiration to try mixing things up.

tracks.name: Subsonic Collision
tracks.desc: Choosing my favorite 808chunk track is like choosing which of your children you love most. 808chunk has always been a great ambassador of Oldskool music and very tight sound design. I love hearing how intricate his tracks are and how well put together they are. His versions of Detroit Techno, Acid Techno, Oldskool Tech/House, and Electro music will always have a soft spot in my heart and is something that I never tire of hearing.

tracks.name: Kiçini Bikini
tracks.desc: I don't know much about Austek other than that he is Turkish, makes incredible tracks, and loves to move between styles of music with almost every release. His tracks always sound polished and easy on the ears. I hope to improve my production values to match with his and one day I hope to be able to download one of his tracks ;)

tracks.name: Guitar
tracks.desc: 5 years ago I was absolutely floored by what yas had created. I remember my browser kept crashing whenever I just tried opening the song and playing it. This one really inspired me to try chopping samples more and is a tactic I currently use in nearly every track. yas, along with the last person in this list were very influential in my musical development.

tracks.name: Levitation
tracks.desc: The first of my two favorite Audiotool junglists. She has a way of using samples, manipulating them, and fitting them seamlessly into a track. This track in particular was such a great example of classic Jungle that it ended up on the

tracks.name: please
tracks.desc: I can't express enough how emotional this track is. It's gotten me through hard times in the past, to be completely honest.

tracks.name: On The Roads
tracks.desc: I love Jazz. This song inspired me to continue to try and incorporate different genres and influences into whatever I write; regardless if it's a chord progression, a bassline, or even just a melodic stab. No nuance is too subtle to impact a song.

tracks.name: No Future (ft. CGMan)
tracks.desc: Just one of those tracks that leaves you awe. I've included it purely on its merits in sound design. Seriously a pleasure to listen to.

tracks.name: BABY LET ME
tracks.desc: One of the tracks that I really fell in love with earlier in my Audiotool journey. It really showed me the beauty in percussion, and how the oft-maligned Audiotool reverb can really shine in the right situations.

tracks.name: River
tracks.desc: Really just amazing atmospheric work. Everything is done so perfectly; I'm still listening to it from time to time.

tracks.name: Proxima
tracks.desc: This brings back so many nostalgic memories of the SEGA Genesis, I can't even explain how happy this track makes me. There'll always be a place for Sonic the Hedgehog's music in my heart.

tracks.name: Opus 1: 'Dubstep Sonata' Mvt. 1 in F# minor
tracks.desc: This is such a cool track. The production was awesome for the time, and the composition was pretty inspiring; it really shows you that harder sounds can fit right in with more melodic ones.

tracks.name: Nocturne
tracks.desc: The power of a well-executed bassline, and a perfect atmosphere.

tracks.name: Rebel.
tracks.desc: I've had the pleasure of working with Kryptic on a few occasions, and what amazes me the most is probably how far he's able to see ahead when he works. Rebel is probably one of my favourites from him; it's just great to listen to. If you don't already, give his stuff a listen!

tracks.name: Coriolis
tracks.desc: > <script type=

tracks.name: Detroit is Calling
tracks.desc: Superb! Besides being a great producer, richyrich makes a great chord stab, inspired by the power of the soul of Detroit Techno.

tracks.name: Extatic Flashback
tracks.desc: The master ... (I do not need to describe the track any more, just listen) Thanks to 808chunk, I decided that I was going to make music on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Scarlett
tracks.desc: Mikel Boyles produces many beautiful and deep tracks, that make you go into a trance.

tracks.name: Bicycle Day
tracks.desc: I love Acid! I remember the tracks of the 90's. ShivaCult is one of the best acid producers on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Neptune
tracks.desc: Zé is a great friend ... those toms, hi hats, synths, drums ... The futurism of this track brings me a feeling that I'm in a Sci-Fi movie.
If you had not put the name of the artist who produced this track, I'd have thought it was a track of the major producer Robert Hood. I have been impressed by ascent's ability since I heard

tracks.name: clock
tracks.desc: Super Neons On Vegas Airwaves

tracks.name: Awsome Swells
tracks.desc: I really like the track and heliotrope's Old School style:)

tracks.name: Interactive Whiteboard | Techno track
tracks.desc: This noise ...

tracks.name: Trapped Inside the Box
tracks.desc: An insanely gorgeous track that tickles my ears in all the right ways. It's just so, so good in every way.

tracks.name: Office Bossa Nova
tracks.desc: All of Frigolitos tracks are astronomically miles above what everyone else on Audiotool can make. Coincidentally, I find this track rather amusing.

tracks.name: Beyond Reality
tracks.desc: A really tallented guy with obviously a lot of experience. Audiotool needs more psytrance. Everything here is pure excellence and shows what audiotool can do.

tracks.name: Too Far
tracks.desc: Love the rhodes in this track. Yeah Astrum has a lot of dance music, but I like his chill stuff.

tracks.name: disco shark
tracks.desc: Awesome, I love the the percussion work. The synths are addictive. I wanna dance in the sea... n_n

tracks.name: Suzlukla
tracks.desc: My first reaction was OH! That sound is really danceable, stupendous rhythm and crazy synths, love it ... n_n

tracks.name: clubbing
tracks.desc: The synths are cool, love the subtle phaser effect. I keep my trance and feel a small sense of dance in me... n_n

tracks.name: Funk experiment
tracks.desc: Brings me old memories. Funk style. The sound of the bass and piano are pleasant but it's the synths that catch me... n_n

tracks.name: LoveBall
tracks.desc: Glorious sound, excellent management of the samples. Nice creative work in the bass, really feel something with this. It's right: No Pulve No Party... n_n

tracks.name: Great Escape
tracks.desc: Feel the energy flowing. This sound is really relaxing for me. n_n

tracks.name: Palm Trees
tracks.desc: I love a lot the Drum 'n' Bass, but this style is impressive. He is a genius with the synths. They are fantastic. It makes me feel good and without a doubt I would call it brilliant... n_n

tracks.name: sister
tracks.desc: 8 bit nostalgic feel. Continuous rhythm, awesome synths. I love the wood block, it makes me shudder. Maybe it is a bit short but I love it! n_n

tracks.name: Faded
tracks.desc: I've got to thank $Ramoney$ for helping me get interested in trap music. This song has some awesome melodies that go perfectly together. Songs like this one make me believe that trap music isn't just repetitive hi hats, 808's, and air horns. This song is a gem.

tracks.name: Doctrina de Lumine
tracks.desc: I remember first listening to this song. I was feeling kinda down and I'm not sure how, but it kinda matched how i was feeling. I loved the percussion. I had never heard anything like it before, and even though it was weird, the song would most likely feel very incomplete without it. The attack on the pad was genius. I just really like the vibe this song gives off.

tracks.name: ixcanxfly.
tracks.desc: Possibly one of the best Lo-Fi Hip Hop songs I've ever heard. Everything about this song is excellent to me. The sampling is great. The distortion is superb. This is a legit [AS] bump track.

tracks.name: Home
tracks.desc: This was one of the first songs I'd heard on audiotool when I first started. At the time I was just getting interested in genres other than regular hip hop. This song sounded so professional. To this day I wonder how DR U M was able to make this masterpiece. This song helped me get interested in making music.

tracks.name: [AS] Magic Lamp
tracks.desc: OBN helped get me interested in experimental hip hop early on. After I heard this song I started discovering artists similar to him outside of audiotool and on audiotool. OBN has made hundreds of other beauties but this one will always have a special place in my mind.

tracks.name: Dream Tape
tracks.desc: This is the best Trance track I have found on Audiotool. When I started listening I immediately fell in love with this amazing masterpiece because of the pad shots he uses which create a really cool ambience. The track develops perfectly and then at 2:51 my brain starts

tracks.name: 4 AM
tracks.desc: Astrum really chose the perfect title for his beautiful track. This masterpiece really gets me because every time I listen to it I feel that I'm actually alone at my desk at 4 a.m. producing in front of a window from which I can see all the city lights. Alone yes, but not completely: because I'm with my friend - which is music.

tracks.name: Airglow
tracks.desc: Another trancey bomb from Kepz. This track like his

tracks.name: Find a place right here
tracks.desc: Amazing collaboration of Cripta and Sandburgen! The melody is fantastic, the vocals are perfect and all the effects get me immediately, Great Masterpiece.

tracks.name: Shine in the Night
tracks.desc: Great collab of Lowfeel and uncle Cripta! Super sounds and melody which develops perfectly (2:41 WOW). The drums are perfect and the acoustic snare which is far from the main beat is just perfect because it gives an amazing feeling of power. Great track! I just hope that the four horsemen appear on Audiotool again.

tracks.name: Beyond Reality
tracks.desc: This track is just BIG! It's a Psytrance masterpiece which immediately penetrates your mind (

tracks.name: there's always tomorrow
tracks.desc: This great artist has amazed everyone with his beautiful tracks and this beautiful piece of art particularly inspired me. The melody is great and the sounds are as clean as they can be. Opaq's track makes me think of a thing that starts and finishes, not a concrete thing but an abstract one. Great track bro.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: I think this is the cleanest set of pads that I ever heard on Audiotool. All the sounds/effects are very professional. This is a track which you hardly believe that it came out of Audiotool. This is a true masterpiece.

tracks.name: Spine
tracks.desc: This track has an amazing melody work, just wait for it. The drums are perfect and the bass is very warm and powerful.

tracks.name: Auf Kurs (2015)
tracks.desc: There's a reason around 2000 people follow Sndbrgn--he's arguably the greatest pound for pound artist on Audiotool. This piece exemplifies the depth, but also the subtle evolution and movement that goes along with every piece he publishes. Like Plastikman with a sense of groove. Just a top notch, brilliant artist.

tracks.name: Small Intermezzo
tracks.desc: Brilliant electro orchestral stuff here.

tracks.name: tears
tracks.desc: Amoeba is just always incredible. This stuff is demanding of the listener, complex and unpredictable. You can hear a sense of classical harmony in just about every piece, and this is no exception.

tracks.name: Ipyfo
tracks.desc: Hanging out with Kepz and Moogfest last year was one of the highpoints of the year. In addition to being a fantastic guy, he's (maybe more importantly) one of the most brilliantly natural musicians on Audiotool. His sense of space and melody is haunting and careful, and he clearly loves synthesizers, not just sound. Kepz belongs in any sensible top 5 list of greatest Audiotool artists, and this song is no exception.

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: Thousands of Audiotools DEMAND to know what evil genius is behind this. The smart money seems to be on frigolito...but it's quickly becoming one of the great unsolved mysteries of Audiotool.

tracks.name: the passers by
tracks.desc: This song zaps whatever part of my brain can't stop listening to Global Goon. Opaqity always has something unusual and wondrous going on, this one's just offbeat and weird and excellent.

tracks.name: [Dem.Naps]
tracks.desc: Khari reminds me a lot of myself, what with how I use my samples and his structure is extremely similar to mine in many songs. The big difference is that he's faster in development and is easily smoother than me. Proud of this dude, I remember when he was just known as 'sampler'.

tracks.name: Water Park Sunday
tracks.desc: Solace impressed me with this one. I've only know him to do DnB until I heard this track. Not only is the lead beautiful, but his drums are tight, the bass is unexpected and on point, and vibe is just chill. This is the type of song you could play in almost setting and it'll still create the tone of the moment.

tracks.name: japanese trapmaster
tracks.desc: I can't say much about kendrick, other than the fact that he makes bangers. Pretty underrated if you ask me. This song, just spells B.A.N.G.E.R.

tracks.name: [Elegance.]
tracks.desc: Dzool is the guru of creating Lofi sounds through audiotool, which I know first hand is tricky. He really knows how to drown his sound in vinyl while keeping it very enjoyable. I l love all of his tracks, but I love his sample play in this one, particularly.

tracks.name: [Hybrid] Heaven []RMX[]
tracks.desc: This was my unknown entry to Lewi's

tracks.name: C.N.C
tracks.desc: Amazing track. I really enjoy the 8bit-ish flavour of it

tracks.name: closed
tracks.desc: A track I listen to a lot in private. A mind clearing track. When it starts the world turns a bit slower ... cheesy.

tracks.name: wedoallthework
tracks.desc: A comparatively recent track on this list but unbelievably good. I especially love the middle part. Dreamy and danceabely hard at the same time.

tracks.name: Hydrogen
tracks.desc: Simple but effective. Love the craziness in this one.

tracks.name: Sword Of Angels
tracks.desc: Lately I've been exploring the mysteries of dark Techno. Unkindness and Murder inspired me to try Techno myself. His tracks always have such driving vibe. This one is one of my favorites from him. The groove is just so great and I love how the bass seems to roll through the track, providing it with a solid foundation. Show him your love guys ;)

tracks.name: Feat. twila.too - Dropping Now
tracks.desc: I could have chosen any track of the one and only Sndbrgn. I simply just love his synths and his layering. It's unmatched in Audiotool in my opinion. I picked this track because it is the one I remember best. The reason for that is because twila.too is Belgian like me :D Nah ... this tune is produced so well and those vocals just make it perfect.

tracks.name: Last Light Jaws Edit
tracks.desc: This list wouldn't be complete without a track from my good friend SharKyyo. I chose this particular track because I really love his guitar-including tunes. His tracks always have a great groove and this one is no exception. I really love how the guitar and the amazing synths create this dark atmospheric, psychedelic feeling, and of course there are the typical SharKyyo style drums. And I have always admired his 303 skills. Keep them coming mate.

tracks.name: Summit
tracks.desc: I found this track by accident. I was working on a track in the app and I wanted to add something on my timeline at the right bottom of my screen when suddenly a notification popped up. So I accidentally clicked on it. It was one of the best miss-clicks I've ever made. Just listen to this beautiful track and let it take you away to divine places.

tracks.name: REsurrection
tracks.desc: I had to add a Tottmaster Drum & Bass track to my list. He doesn't know it but ever since I joined Audiotool, I have been a big fan of his and of his tracks. Not so long ago I found out that he is also Belgian. So that explains why he is such a nice guy :) About the track ... It's a typical Tottenhauser dark Drum & Bass track but for some reason I like this one even more than his other work. Like in all of his tracks he's showing off his supremacy in drum manipulation. Also the reese in this track is damn hot. And I love the energy that comes out of this one. Let's make sure Audiotool doesn't forget us Belgians ;)

tracks.name: AeneA
tracks.desc: Maybe my favorite non-Drum & Bass track on Audiotool. This track is also one of those tracks that left me sitting in front of my laptop with my mouth wide open, drooling all over my keyboard. I love the big productions of my (really nice) sea-creature-buddy but this one is more than big ... it's gigantic. The drums are dope and all the sounds and noises coming from like everywhere lift this masterpiece to perfection. Make more of this mate. We need tracks like these ... oh ... and you still owe me a new keyboard :)

tracks.name: Mechanical eyes
tracks.desc: I had a hard time deciding if I would pick a Drum & Bass track or another genre. I always make music that I like. And to be honest I like most of my tracks (even the noob-ones). Most of them have a special meaning to me and were created to describe a certain feeling/emotional state I was in at that time. Funnily enough, this track wasn't dictated by any feeling. I just wanted to create an industrial feeling track. I think the intro makes that clear :) I like this track mainly because of this intro (spend a lot of time on that). I also like the energy and anger released in the drop and the fact that I was able to add a little melody (not so obvious for me). In the middle of the track it chills down a little to build up to the end drop with a bit of drum madness at the end. Hope u guys enjoy it as well.

tracks.name: Aaron + Nominal - Everything I want is far away
KTN-01
tracks.desc: This track has so much raw energy and all the samples fit together so well. 3. After Glow (Jonjon Remix) by @jonjon .

tracks.name: Watashi Zone
tracks.desc: This is such a gorgeous track. Even after years I always come back to this track. This was on the old Audiotool website and was featured on the homepage. Also, Zone is one of the nicest guys on the site and had many a good chat with him. Zone also helps me judge Audiotool day J

tracks.name: Cadmium Hotel
tracks.desc: This track’s synth design is easily some of the best on the website. This collaboration by Flying Baby Seal and CGman never fails to impress me.

tracks.name: Flake
tracks.desc: This track’s sound design is incredible. Every sound is perfectly timed and placed in the mix. A lot can be learnt from Olaf’s sound design!

tracks.name: ./dream
tracks.desc: This song is so beautiful. It's so well composed and the vocals fit perfectly alongside everything else. After hearing it once I knew I had to remix it, so there’s a remix on my Soundcloud.

tracks.name: Sole Survivor
tracks.desc: This track was featured back on the old Audiotool website and I’ve never forgotten it. It is so “big” and it's almost like an orchestra of synthesizers.

tracks.name: Dark Dub
tracks.desc: This is an extremely old school, dark Dub-Step track. Infyuthsion released it when everyone was trying to make over the top growls and massive drops, so this track was a breath of fresh air.

tracks.name: EVERYDAY ANYONE
tracks.desc: This track is incredible. The large reverb on every synth makes this track sound incredibly deep. The composition is fairly simple but it has been executed very professionally.

tracks.name: Ursa Minor
tracks.desc: I have a soft spot for this track. It's so old now and it's not good by any modern audiotool standard. But this track was the first track of mine to get featured and is responsible for why I continued producing to this day.

tracks.name: Running from the Wind
tracks.desc: There are just some artists on Audiotool that, unfortunately, don't get enough attention, despite how many followers they have. Perhaps they were forgotten, I dunno, but Xtraction shouldn't be one of those artists. His music is amazingly unique and inspiring. It takes an amazing artist to pull of what he does with minimal activity in the app too, it's pretty amazing what he's accomplished.

tracks.name: Lost In Time
tracks.desc: I believe Dollavix is misunderstood on the part of the Audiotool community. He's a really talented artist and although he uses other explicit elements from other artist's tracks, he still manages to put his own little twist on his work. He's getting better and I believe that with a nudge in the right direction, he can become something great too.

It was only recently that I heard TheAnonymousPineapple's works. Getting to know him better as an artists, we have quite a bit in common in terms of knowledge of music theory, (even though I think he knows much more than I do). This is a pretty good track on his part, nothing like I could make, of course.

tracks.name: Silence In Tokyo
tracks.desc: I haven't really had a chance to just sit down and listen to Audiotool music and find more artists, however, recently, joining in on a few rabbits in the past I was able to find this prodigy who has a ton of amazing works coming in the future. He deserves to be on this list more than I can say.

tracks.name: To Feel Alright (Feat.ENY)
tracks.desc: This is simply a breathtaking track. I only recently heard this, but I'm no good with working with vocals, and I can sense the hard work put into this track. I can definitely see orcean going far with music and look forward to hearing his future work.

tracks.name: kitsch
tracks.desc: RNZR always delivers, but this track just edges out all of his other stuff. Those chords and the percs are absolutely on point.

tracks.name: Liquid Drum and Bass 3
tracks.desc: This track changed the way that I thought about making D&B. It was so insanely smooth and almost nostalgic while still keeping the energy that I loved from Drum and Bass as well. This was my main inspiration making Technicolour, and is still in the rotation on my phone like 3 years after I first heard it.

tracks.name: Blue Days feat. Emma
tracks.desc: Quality Deep House from cihangir. Love the vocals as well.

tracks.name: shudder [IN NAME ONLY EP Nov 15, for real this time]
tracks.desc: NOMINAL has always had some really interesting tracks, but I love the nostalgic feel that this track has, with the main chords sounding distant but always present. Insane stuff.

tracks.name: Find a place right here
tracks.desc: I love the 80’s vibe of this track. It’s a bona fide AT classic.

tracks.name: Love In An Empty Shell (Mikel Boyles Mix)
young
tracks.desc: Proper lovely stuff. Amoeba combines his natural ear for melody with a slightly harder edge than a usual amoeba track, and it pays off really really well.

tracks.name: Big Narstie - Gasleak (FSC remix)
tracks.desc: Don't judge. This goes in hard.

tracks.name: find me
tracks.desc: I love the melody/bass combo, and the drums are decent as well.

tracks.name: Jonjon - Breach of Trust (Akimbo and Jambams future Jungle Remix)
Hue
tracks.desc: Also one of the most underrated artists on Audiotool. Love his beats. Mostly when they are percussive, with those nice wood sounds. like in

tracks.name: Passive
tracks.desc: This track is one of my all time favorites. Feels like it came out yesterday but it's already two years old. This track shows that even a simple track can have a lot of power.

tracks.name: Sunloop
tracks.desc: I have listened to this track 500 times AT LEAST !!!!! On long Bus trips it helps me to sleep. It always warms my heart with the beautiful guitar.

tracks.name: BGN {OVR VIP)
tracks.desc: I Love the beat, the bass, the Stuttered Synth and the Dry snare. Everything fits perfectly together.

tracks.name: Nor
tracks.desc: This is a shout out to all of the other members of our collab. They motivated me most in the last months. This one, definitely is my favorite Untrust track.

tracks.name: Let me take you away
tracks.desc: He is my favorite Ambient/Chill artist on Audiotool. The crackle in combination with the percs and the whisper... I love it <3

tracks.name: there's always tomorrow
tracks.desc: A really long and great Intro. The drop with the Kalimba is even better.

tracks.name: Electronica [Double run]
tracks.desc: I listened to this last week. So underrated!

tracks.name: ultraviolet
tracks.desc: Opaqity is one of my favorite AT artists. I don't understand how he makes his tracks so smooth and jazzy while claiming to know nothing of jazz composition. From the moment I heard his tracks, the sheer classiness of Opaqity's productions immediately blew me away. His attention to detail and worshipful approach to melody and harmony never fails to impress.

tracks.name: conga_jams_01
tracks.desc: Another super raw track from another of AT's finest outsiders. The use of noise and ambiance in this track is so good it hurts. The way the kick and the distorted toms duck that main loop is just ridiculous. Lo-fi at it's lo-finest. VLTRA's got that

tracks.name: tijuana
tracks.desc: Torn (as is the case for many people, I think) was one of the first

tracks.name: Night Train
tracks.desc: One of my earliest fav's as an AT user. The way that delirious bass-line pulls the groove along is incredible. The delicate use of space in this track is perfect. For me, the

tracks.name: Finding the words to say to you
chill tribe vibes interlude 10m Boiler Mix
tracks.desc: Finally the great Lewi-Astro. This track is just way too cool for school. Perfect mixing proportions, so much detail, amazing use of repetition and subtle manipulation. Definitely one of the chillest tracks on AT, this beast clocks out at 12:42 and feels more like a short set than a single track. I'd also like to take a moment to thank Lewi for the amazing sample collection that he has made available to the AT community.

tracks.name: Hydrogen
tracks.desc: I don't know if this dude's still making tracks, but he/she/it has a bunch already that are excellent. I especially love the idea of tracks inspired by the periodic table. Support the AT underground, check it out!

tracks.name: semiquaver
tracks.desc: I've been doing a lot of 4 on the floor lately, felt like switching things up a bit. I'm really happy with the marriage of the kick drum and bass in this track, wish there was a little more action in the air-bands though...

tracks.name: Transoceanic
tracks.desc: This tune transports a lot of energy and mood. I think this track of The Seahorse really shows his potential and his wide musical background…

tracks.name: Royal Flush
tracks.desc: This track is so powerful and magnetic that I had to hear it lots of times. Even though it’s not my favorite music genre…

tracks.name: OBJeCT
tracks.desc: This track got a beautiful atmosphere and it’s constructed quite interestingly. Furthermore it uses effects in a perfect and effective way, especially the reverb…

tracks.name: The Truth
tracks.desc: Oh my good – what a track! It shows perfectly, that you can create real professional sounding Psy-Trance on Audiotool! What a pity that Sonic Particle isn't publishing at the moment.

tracks.name: flatliners
tracks.desc: A powerful track full of pressure! For the most percussions and drums he used synthesizer and not samples – a quite interesting sound…

tracks.name: Meerbusch
tracks.desc: This is my latest earworm on Audiotool! It sounds really magnetic and shows the art how to make minimal music that never sounds boring, even if the same melody runs through the whole song…

tracks.name: Geometric Abstraction
tracks.desc: He is in my opinion the most underrated musician on

tracks.name: evolutionary humanism
tracks.desc: This is not the best track of mine, but it's message seems very important to me! Currently very bad and inhuman things are going on in Germany and Europe… Again hostility toward strangers can be seen and I really don’t want to support this – because: we are one!!!

tracks.name: silver palm
tracks.desc: Description: One word I can use to describe opaqity’s tracks: Percs. Most of opaq’s songs are pretty good but this would have to be my favorite from him. I honestly really liked the percs he used in this one. Let’s not forget about the rest though. The chords, the melody that plays in the track overtime, and even the white noise. I really loved this track when I first heard it <3.

tracks.name: Ubongo
tracks.desc: Anything from CGman just proves what Audiotool is capable of doing. Every song he makes has some pretty crazy atmospheres and Neuro basses. Every time I hear one of them, I am amazed. I honestly love CGman’s work, and I’m always excited to hear what he has coming up next.

tracks.name: Contraflux
tracks.desc: This was one of the first songs I heard on Audiotool. It’s what got me into Electro House stuff. Lux’s Electro House was something I truly miss, but his new stuff is pretty good. Hope to see more from him soon.

tracks.name: Purple
tracks.desc: I sometimes listen to chill sort of stuff. I really enjoyed this when I first heard it. The pads. The bass. Teqtoniq’s stuff amazes me everytime. Hope to see him get into medical school soon! :D

tracks.name: bloom
tracks.desc: I’ve known Neo for quite a bit of time, and I’ve always enjoyed hearing his stuff every single time I hear it. But I’ve never been able to picture him making chill music for once. I quite honestly didn’t expect to hear anything like this from him, and I loved it from the very first second. The drums, the melody, and let’s not forget the arp <3

tracks.name: LIMITLESS
tracks.desc: Of all songs I’ve heard from this guy I instantly fell in love with this one. The supersaws, the lead, the little arp that plays at some parts of the track. I couldn’t get over this song the first time I heard it. But go a bit easy on the compression next time BSRK! :)))) <3

tracks.name: Lost
tracks.desc: Well deserved winner of the Moogfest Contest. This track also raises the justifiable question: how the hell did he get all this out of only two Pulverisateurs? Kepz also would have been on any list of mine. His stuff is not only very well produced, it also meets my taste in 99.9% of cases.

tracks.name: move
tracks.desc: There is no 100% pure Audiotool list without Mr Standfast. In very few of his tracks are there any signs of samples. His synth melodies are unmatched not to mention what he does with the drums. That's Mr Standfast.

tracks.name: Verlangen
tracks.desc: One of my first real favorites on Audiotool (and what luck without samples). A perfect symbiosis of a cool Minimal beat and atmospheric synth lines. And that bass! And it was here that I first understood the Tonematrix as an respectable production tool not just a toy. We can only hope for a Sumad comeback soon.

tracks.name: Nowhere But Everywhere
tracks.desc: Freshest addition to the list: heartwarming Trance synth melodies and ... isn't that a choir? No that's impossible isn't it. In the middle of the track we are surprised to hear something like a little organ solo which conjures up a smile and then sends us back to the dreamy melancholia of this piece of art.

tracks.name: Strange Small Blue Box Dub
tracks.desc: We don't have to say anything about Noraus' qualities as a sound designer. What he has created here without samples or even a drum computer is mind-blowing. The glitchy hi hats, the percussions and the fat kick drum, all synthesized with the Heisenberg is just indescribable. And on top of it all it is simply an awesome Dub track.

tracks.name: dark melody (fbs mix)
tracks.desc: What FBS has done with @enjoyyourshoes ' small but no less beautiful Tonematrix melody is remarkable. When the Rasslebock adds the bass, the dark sun rises.

tracks.name: ethereal
tracks.desc: Great Neo-Trance piece without a single sample. Christian Chrom has some of this kind on his tracklist.

tracks.name: Photomultiplier
tracks.desc: Another sound master: Tyburn. Here too no trace of drum computer. Instead 100% Pulverisateur. A stroke of genius in my eyes. A little more Tyburn wouldn't hurt in future.

tracks.name: one pulver only
tracks.desc: One Pulve, one reverb and a compressor. Not the setup you would expect behind this fresh and funky milestone. This is really worth opening, there is something to learn here.

tracks.name: Deep Inside
tracks.desc: A monster of a downtempo track. Brutal und inexorable it rolls on and takes no prisoners.

tracks.name: second guess
tracks.desc: Just as with Luxior and a number of other young Audiotool talents, it is hard to believe opaqity’s age after you see his 100+ track library of some of the best music on Audiotool. Here, opaqity went in a less expected direction, deciding to ditch the beat and push the sounds of percussion into the background while focusing on the powerful pads, a gentle melody and immersive ambience. A type of music that is fairly rare on Audiotool.

tracks.name: We'll make it through
tracks.desc: It is no surprise even Joel himself had moments of regret since deleting his main page with his work from the last 4+ years. His beautiful, experimental and expressive music, poetic and open personality made him one of the most interesting users on the website. Continuing on a new chapter ‘sky bound zoo’, predominantly with heavy manipulation of samples, he delivers incredibly rich, densely packed and complicated compositions.

tracks.name: rain
tracks.desc: To stick within such well defined boundaries of a genre and to create something so unique, alive, imagery evoking is a work of pure talent. Percussion and noise morphing into the sounds of water, deep echoing chords mimicking the feeling of distant thunder are just a few aspects making it a masterpiece. The track unpredictably but gently unravels and progresses like a passing night storm, and still remains danceable enough for a techno set in a club.

tracks.name: SURF DUDE
tracks.desc: VLTRA has been perfecting his unique sound over the last couple of years and it is no surprise he has become successful outside of Audiotool, releasing his first record as TLIM SHUG on Echovort Records and having it sold out on several online stores within days. His touch of messy lo-fi beats, deep basslines, dreamy and surreal vibes and clever ideas in every track placed him as my favourite artist this and previous year.

tracks.name: Sandstorm
tracks.desc: Sandburgen was one of the earliest users to show why Audiotool should be taken seriously. His music sounds rich and is usually melody driven with a minimalistic structure letting the user focus on subtle changes and progressions. ‘Sandstorm’ has messier and heavier sound design than most of his work, but here it works very well. Planned to be released as part of Sandorama record, it never made the cut being difficult to re-master, becoming a forgotten classic.

tracks.name: kitsch
tracks.desc: Another oldie that is still impressing me almost 4 years later. rnzr is known for pushing the boundaries of Audiotool in the early days, putting out music of mind-blowing quality. Open up his projects and you’ll see a mess of tools and timeline regions that is hard to make sense of. The hidden secret behind his well crafted and polished music with high attention to detail in every layer.

tracks.name: Realmvision '93
tracks.desc: Created by the same person behind the VLTRA alias it definitely has aspects of his recognizable style, only this time bumping the bpm up to 140 and with a careful selection of samples, he created one of his most powerful, nostalgia evoking tracks. I still can’t get enough of this piece and it is without a doubt my favorite track of 2015.

Semitama really should be the “underrated artist” of this list having just over 60 followers, but their consistently fresh, creative and quality output landed them in my top 10 inspirations. Semitama’s unique work comes like breaths of fresh air in the track feed and never fails to impress and make you ponder the mystery behind this silent user.

tracks.name: Emancipation
tracks.desc: cihangir makes the best Deep House in the place, for me at least. This track is a perfect example of his art of mixing. Smooth synths with cool vox and great percs, with delicately slight fx for a cozy atmosphere, taking your mind far far away.

tracks.name: touch e
tracks.desc: Another talented producer, with a wide range of styles. This is a fast ride on the 303 highway. A bit repetitive? Yes maybe, but so gooood!!!

tracks.name: Acid Burst
tracks.desc: This guy always makes great tracks with crystal clear production! This one is a good example of his ability to create deep Acid tunes. But not only out of a 303. A real master of this machine here on AT.

tracks.name: Plane
