tracks.desc: Shoutout to my friends at Untrust, this one was really a team effort, and I'm very happy with how it turned out. We have plenty of finished/nearly finished material, so keep watching us in 2016! We're desperate for Next though.

tracks.name: Ømega
tracks.desc: A no-brainer. Just open it up, look at the synthwork, and kneel in awe to the C-god.

tracks.name: Underground Lake
tracks.desc: Kepz, also one of my favourite artists. I really value production and the amount of work put into details, and this is a great example again. Kepz somehow makes his reverb sound different, and WAY better than everybody elses reverb. The drums and melody are also very creative.

tracks.name: Corner Shop
tracks.desc: I remember when this was featured. I listened to it, was like

tracks.name: D u n k e l
tracks.desc: Aaron makes such innovative beats. Amazing artist, amazing textures. Very unique style!

tracks.name: Ripples (Luxior chill edit)
tracks.desc: Luxior has some sort of raw talent that I haven't seen anywhere else. Melodically super cool, drum-work always on point, and how the heck did he make the chords at 3:52 sound like that?!

tracks.name: abs x lux - peripherals
tracks.desc: Shoutout to my boy Abstract, his melodies are insane, and the variation this tune in particular has is just incredible!

tracks.name: ides of march
tracks.desc: There are no words to describe how beautiful this song is. Tornsage uses a steady beat and combines vocals and pianos to create the perfect mix.

tracks.name: Focus
tracks.desc: Mikke created such a soul-touching song with this one. This inspired me to progress further when collaborating with Notoz.

tracks.name: Distant.
tracks.desc: Kryptic started to reach out and find his potential in music last summer. This track was a good example of music progression on AT.

tracks.name: Underground Love (Ft. DubLion)
tracks.desc: This collab was the start of something special. Xav wanted to see what would happen when two styles of music production come together. I'm glad this collaboration happened.

tracks.name: Metropolis
tracks.desc: This song has inspired me from the start. Nostalgia is what is brought back for Ketram‘s old songs. Ketram has always been an Audiotool legend to me.

tracks.name: Stream
tracks.desc: This song takes a darker turn to any mix. He always delivers the best of Neuro Drum and Bass when are in need of it.

tracks.name: spiral
tracks.desc: So many memories with this one. The melody, the bass, the atmosphere. Really just an overall beautiful track that I often come back to. Amoeba has many other amazing tracks, but this one hits the feels.

tracks.name: Into the atmosphere
tracks.desc: Another artist who inspired me to move towards Drum and Bass. Sota has many other great tracks so I suggest those who haven't checked him out should really do so.

tracks.name: Day Dream
tracks.desc: Dublion is without a doubt a talented artist. I've been working with him for some time now and I'm pretty impressed with the amount of motivation this guy has. This is personally my favorite track from him.

tracks.name: Waves In The Sunset
tracks.desc: Syntax' pads are incredible and in this track really shows it. A really great track made by Syntax, absolutely love the simplicity of this track.

tracks.name: Broken Projector
tracks.desc: Kepz is a master at his art, whether it be music or painting (yes I saw that Pulv painting of yours). This track by Kepz is my personal favorite, like a lot of the tracks on here, it brings back memories.

tracks.name: Comedown
tracks.desc: Another one. This along with Heis'n'b brings back so many memories. I remember this was my first track I ever downloaded at Audiotool around my freshman year of High school. A great classic by Astrum.

tracks.name: Re-boot the system
tracks.desc: I've always been a fan of Tyburn, he has musical golden thumbs, and this proved it. I wouldn't even know what to classify this as, but it's chaotic and it works so well.

tracks.name: Soar
tracks.desc: As my comment on the actual track says, I still maintain this is my personal favorite ambient track on AT. I don't know how he made that big gliding lead and I don't want to know, just to keep the magic. This has been one of my 'comfort tracks' over the last few years. Just wish I was around when Won Ton was still active.

tracks.name: Chirp
tracks.desc: Don't get me wrong, for me and many others (I assume, look at that follower count man) Xav is one of my favorite artists, but the sheer uniqueness of this track makes it memorable. It's all calm, birds chirping, some water fx, then bam, one of the fattest Neuro/Glitch basses I've ever heard.

tracks.name: #FDR002 Hearts
tracks.desc: This is one of those tracks that from the second I pressed play, I just sat motionless in my chair and listened, my mind playing out like a forlorn black and white film. A thinking track, but still a massive favorite.

tracks.name: Malice
tracks.desc: Feel a bit bad for putting the same artist in here twice, but c'mon it's Tyburn. This was the first time I'd heard AT being used for UK-style Dubstep. The vibe Tyburn creates in this is still something I'm trying to emulate.

tracks.name: Technicolour
tracks.desc: Akimbo and I started out on AT at pretty similar times, and I like to think both of our sounds have matured and evolved during that time. This hands down is my fave of his though. The first time I listened my first thought was 'Yup, he's defo going places'

tracks.name: Nervous
tracks.desc: My under-rated artist is Jaradodo for sure, always been a solid presence ever since I stepped into AT. It's always a gamble as to what genre they put out, but it's always a charm to listen.

tracks.name: Undercharge
tracks.desc: Beautifully dark and atmospheric

tracks.name: GRIK6:)!
tracks.desc: Well, very mysterious piece. Awesome!

tracks.name: tuna una
tracks.desc: Really nice Dub-Techno but unfortunately the only track by this artist.

tracks.name: SURF DUDE
tracks.desc: Yes, very nice Oldschool as well

tracks.name: Seltsames Objekt
tracks.desc: Good, nice Dub-Techo with plenty of interference, love it.

tracks.name: Inner Mask Disorder
tracks.desc: Schranz at its best. So many variations masterfully transitioned, how can anyone not be inspired to dabble in this genre? Must turn my hand to it again.

tracks.name: Blown (mind is gone)
tracks.desc: Couldn’t tell you the genre, I just love the track. For me, though the bass is awesome, it’s the vocals that make this a killer. Quite like the Hip-Hop influences in this too.

tracks.name: simply sound DUB
tracks.desc: Other than that sweet low bass, I really think the grime-vibe sounds in there are sweet. Really eerie track. I feel this is how melodic Dubstep should sound. Way cooler sound.

tracks.name: Survival
tracks.desc: This was the first CGMan track I opened up and subsequently copied the bass design. I have since gone on to use a set-up similar to these in pretty much all Neuro style tracks I do. I have now made a template track of the basic set-up for this...such a time-saver.

tracks.name: Yesterday
tracks.desc: Couldn’t resist. In fairness, I do like almost anything this guy makes. This was the track he came back with after a break, best comeback ever. Nailed it.

tracks.name: Genera (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: Such a fun, up-beat track. Pretty cool, if simple, sound design. Great percussion work too. Listened to this kind of thing loads before finally turning to dnb. Never could make it though. We started a collab outside of AT a long time ago..I really should go back and work on it again.

tracks.name: Flake
tracks.desc: There is no one track that stands out from Olaf, I like his Garage, his tech and his Ambient. But this one is something I find quite inspiring. The composition is sound, the sounds haunting and the journey is sublime. When thinking ambient, this is where I turn.

tracks.name: The Eyes
tracks.desc: Nothing about this I don’t like. Maybe one day I will remix it and turn it darker. Maybe.

tracks.name: miss with me RMX (WIP)
tracks.desc: I always liked Tott‘s (and Oedi’s) darker Drum and Bass stuff. This remix is lighter than his usual Drum and Bass, but dark and filthy. I could probably attribute my first inclination to produce Drum and Bass to him and Oedi, with a nod to Jonjon too. I hope he comes back to Drum and Bass one day.

tracks.name: R2-DTune
tracks.desc: Only a Mad Scientist can make music like this

tracks.name: sunrays
tracks.desc: A perfect House groove with NO samples , Respect.

tracks.name: That's What They Want You to Think
A Place for Us
tracks.desc: Really just too cool for words... Just plug in and fly!

tracks.name: Little Box of Joy
tracks.desc: The Trancemeister at his best. Boogie on down to Frisco and Dance all night to this California Boy.

tracks.name: Engineer
tracks.desc: Glitch-hop at its very best by the Master Engineer himself.

tracks.name: Tsunami
tracks.desc: One of the most exciting tracks on A.T ever. Just sends me crazy Man ... Love it:

tracks.name: Clara's Eyes
tracks.desc: This Glitched Orchestral of mine tipyfies my style and uses samples from my StonehengeS tone Guitar. http://stonehengeguitars.com/

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: ONE 303 and an absurd amount of fx was needed to make this beautiful track. I spend a few hours in the draft but still haven't a clue how this is possible :) For sure one of AT's best tracks. I am sure many people are still waiting for another track from Someone

tracks.name: smoked
tracks.desc: Tracks 1 and 2 were not influenced by my personal music taste so you could basically count this as nr1. I really like Drumcode rec. a lot and this track could easily be on that label. Olondro , in my opinion you are a Scandinavian, at least in your music style.

tracks.name: GRIK6:)!
tracks.desc: N1m8:)! Awesome track yafee? we want more!!! 7. Psychose by @song1

tracks.name: Underbred
tracks.desc: Known for his real good Neuro-Drum and Bass skills E-trim also has also made a few Techno tracks and I really like what he does.

tracks.name: The God Particle ( Christian-Chrom and The Seahorse )
tracks.desc: Shame on me to put in a track I worked on but it's still a favorite and stylewise something new. I am realyl proud of this track and The Seahorse did such a good job on it.

tracks.name: Future Cities
tracks.desc: In my opinion Ayearty really deserves more attention because he is amazing. Great synth-design and atmosphere in his tracks. Check him out!

tracks.name: dark rite
tracks.desc: I selected this track as my favorite of my own. There are a few others that I like but this one is the closest to my personal music taste.

tracks.name: Euphoria Crash!
tracks.desc: JamBam one of my early friends here, plays with 303 a lot, nice bro!

tracks.name: damage
tracks.desc: Tornsage, very very good music from this great man. Very beautifull music. A must listen!

tracks.name: ecole
tracks.desc: Mr.Standfast, don't know him that well but love his music. Keep it up bro!

tracks.name: Inner Out
tracks.desc: 808chunk, one of the first I heard on here.

tracks.name: autoscontro
tracks.desc: Cripta, one of the very best around. Great guy. We met in Belgium, good times my Italian friend.

tracks.name: kitsch
tracks.desc: Rnzr, also a great guy, met him in Belgium too at the AT party. Real musician/DJ, love your stuff mate!

tracks.name: The Awakening
tracks.desc: One of the few Melbourne bounce songs I've heard on Audiotool, and one of the best. Very well produced and the lead synth was on point. The whole time I couldn't stop myself from bobbing my head.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - n0maD$
tracks.desc: This has to be one of the more interesting tracks that I've heard, but I love it. The rhythms he uses are very interesting and kept me intrigued. And His percussion work is A1.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - MyNameIsChuckles
tracks.desc: A personal favorite of mine when I need something to chill out to. I've many times fallen asleep to this song. Very atmospheric and great percussion work, as well.

tracks.name: Tsunami
tracks.desc: The track just goes hard and I just love it. I usually play it when I do shows.

tracks.name: Cluster I
tracks.desc: Another track I usually relax to. I love that smooth, sliding synth and those space-esque chords. Again, great percussion work.

tracks.name: first_work (edit/extension)
tracks.desc: I really do not know what to say about this except the fact that I love it oh so much. The original is great, but I love all the additions that Dove added.

tracks.name: Aarons Rush
tracks.desc: Ok, this song really hits me emotionally. I was going through all of Darklite's tracks and stumbled upon this one. Though the mastering could be better, throughout the whole song I could feel the emotion that Darklite must've been feeling. Absolutely great piece.

tracks.name: You Found Me
tracks.desc: This song is my all time favorite song on Audiotool. It is in my music library and I listen to it nearly everyday. This song puts me in a state of mind that I can't explain, and brings me somewhere else. Amazing.

tracks.name: what if they take your head off and turn you into a cop for there dirty work?
Arrival
tracks.desc: Was an honor to be the first person to favorite this track by an artist unknown to the community and then see it as a featured track a few days later. An amazing song that I keep going back to over and over again. And an AT classic.

tracks.name: All Of My Friends (Julsy Remix)
tracks.desc: Another guitar-heavy track, both Julsy and Sky Bound Zoo on the same track is not an uncommon thing, but this remix is so beautiful it keeps me coming back, percs are a fav!

tracks.name: Underwater Trees
tracks.desc: Probably my favorite AT track. Two powerhouses of skill and perfect sound design, Zone and Minotaur team up and make a track that makes me feel so inspired to make something half as good. This song reminds me of a car ride where you reflect on all the trials that you’ve gone through to get where you stand today. The good and the bad…and accepting your finite existence. From the percs to the mystifying pad, this beautiful song structure keeps me coming back again and again (just look at my comments on the track)

tracks.name: Elm Street
tracks.desc: A cinematic and gorgeous work by Uprising who gives so much to his music, the transitions are beautiful and the atmosphere is so cultivating from the needle-like high hats to the plucks.

tracks.name: Tidal
tracks.desc: Tyburn has been the person who I’ve learned from the most. I am constantly opening his tracks to get insight on how to make sound with such clarity. Really, any one of his tracks would have made the cut. I love the delay effects and the synth from the beginning here. Such powerful sound.

tracks.name: Twilatoo - Je rave (the schedule remix)
tracks.desc: ... by The Schedule also known as Sandburgen (now Nock Nilte) I remember where I was when I listened to this track for the first time. An amazing track that blew me away from start to finish.

tracks.name: 3- EVERYDAY ANYONE [2013 mix]
tracks.desc: An AT classic. This track has constantly haunted me throughout the years the way the Lengendary Pink Dot’s Alchemical Playschool (highly recommended-but beware!) https://legendarypinkdots1.bandcamp.com/album/alchemical-playschool) has haunted me. An amazing track with beautiful bass and contagious dark groove.

tracks.name: Ex-Data
tracks.desc: Intracktion is another producer whose music comes from the depths of the heart. In particular, the tracks he sings over give me the shivers. This particular track shines because of its unique groove, the nice melodies and the perfect drum work.

tracks.name: Cembalo undead [Orchester der Struktur Remix]
tracks.desc: Structure/Sandburgens distinctive sound never gets boring. He manages to capture many different moods and still keep his unique style. This track is a weird mixture of melancholic strings with chirpy melodies. A truly enjoyable trip.

tracks.name: LION VERSUS SNAKE  - ROCKSTEADY DUB REGGAE
Slapstick Speakeasy
tracks.desc: Sota creates wonderfully grooving Bass Music. His percussion work is some of the most outstanding I’ve heard on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Reincarnation - Yerevan (Zend Dubstep Remix)
tracks.desc: This track by Triquend isn’t the style of dubstep I’m usually into. The beautiful original track and really well designed synths and the overall progression of this piece made it stick in my head. One I’ve been coming back to frequently.

tracks.name: Friday afternoon jam feat Christian Chrom
tracks.desc: Two magnificent producers packed into one track here. Christian Chrom who masters experimental Audiotool-only production and SharkYyo with his psychedelic and progressive style. I really enjoy the euphoric melody and the raw bass on this one.

tracks.name: Rägeboge!
tracks.desc: Pathfinder packs tons of energy in his tracks. On top of that he manages to produce a really professional sound. This is one of my faves of his because it’s the only track on Audiotool I know of with Swiss German vocals ;)

tracks.name: Chalk
tracks.desc: Ed Knowles is a very versatile producer. I especially like his breakbeat pieces like this one. He’s way too unknown considering the quality of his compositions so go and check him out!

tracks.name: Dubby Shizzle (feat. KernelPanic)
tracks.desc: Digging up an old one here. This is a special one to me because I worked on it with my mate who showed me Audiotool. It’s rather slow progressing. I like the spacey sound, the piano like chords and the drum beat on this one.

tracks.name: Chill n' Bass [Full Track]
tracks.desc: Infyuthsion has always had a high quality of tracks and this one was one of my first listens to his music.

tracks.name: My Radio Is Jammin' On
tracks.desc: One of my favorites on the site. Just a jammin' track!

tracks.name: Illuminated Darkness
tracks.desc: 808Chunk has been a huge influence to me on the site and I try to learn as much as I can from his music.

tracks.name: 7/4 DnB
tracks.desc: Another oldie, but well done track.

tracks.name: Parlez-vous Francais
tracks.desc: A great filtered track by Joa. A classic!

tracks.name: Farewell, Friends.
tracks.desc: This track really makes me smile. I love the sounds in it.

tracks.name: Footprints in technicolor
tracks.desc: Sbronz is so epic , he is one of the old users and I just love his enery. This track has everything.

tracks.name: Industrial Sabotage
tracks.desc: Cripta's music really gets me. I love the deep and dark feel he puts into his tracks.

tracks.name: Stanze
tracks.desc: This heavy work by CGMAN is so damned good, really like the big industrial sounds and the pounding kick.

tracks.name: Night Run
tracks.desc: I think if this were the wake up track for everyone in the world poeple would be a lot more relaxed. I would have a smile all day long.

tracks.name: Dirty Dan Pt .2 (Party Daze) FT. Oedipax & DJ Simplex
Til you
tracks.desc: A collaboration of two people that I look up to and respect so much. These two are intelligent producers who can work well together. I want to have a career where I can work well with people like these guys.

tracks.name: remnant
tracks.desc: There is so much in this track that makes it unique. The chord progression and melody complement each other in ways that I can't describe. The percussion is laid back and simple, yet has a drive to it that makes you want to keep listening.

tracks.name: Distorted Memories
tracks.desc: From a longtime Audiotool friend of mine. This track demonstrates part of how far along he's come and how talented he is. The sound design is diverse as well as each part of the track being important.

tracks.name: Synthesizer Symphony 1st Mov.
tracks.desc: A track I discovered when I first joined Audiotool. It reflects a little bit of me in it since I have a classical background as well as having electronic influences.

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: The reason why this track inspires me so much is that the source of every sound comes from one 303, which makes me believe that anything is possible when it comes to producing music. Not only does every sound come from one 303 but the balance sounds incredible. It feels like I'm listening to a story when listening it. I couldn't imagine the amount of time and effort put into this.

tracks.name: Panopticon
tracks.desc: One of the tracks that caused me to develop my love for Drum and Bass. It reflects a lot about the DnB scene in Seattle, Washington.

tracks.name: Black
tracks.desc: A simple, yet complex track. This track reminds me that less is more in a lot of cases of music.

tracks.name: [Title]
tracks.desc: From a good friend of mine who inspires me to keep producing music and to do my best to become a better producer. I get reminded of all of the friends I've made through Audiotool each time I listen to this track.

tracks.name: 火花
tracks.desc: This track reminds me of the song playing on the welcome screen on TV at a hotel room, and I really like the way the guitar is chopped in it.

tracks.name: unrest
tracks.desc: I love trrrrillionaire and wish he hadn't deleted so many amazing tracks. Great musician here.

tracks.name: neotokyodub
tracks.desc: VLTRA is a god. His music is too good... Makes me jealous. Such raw emotion; I never thought electronic music could be this deep before finding his tracks. All hi tracks are so deep and this is no exception... How does he make these pads? I've been trying to find out for like a year and still don't know. This album could easily just be 10 of his tracks.

tracks.name: the wound
tracks.desc: Before this track I have to admit I wrote off luxior because a lot of his tracks didn't resonate with me, but I really need to give him kudos for this one. So nice how the bass provides motion through the track, that way it goes up a half step w/ the stuff over top remaining the same reminds me so much of the music I played in the jazz band in high school... Even though I don't like his style I have to say he's great at music and has a lot of knowledge and can't wait to see how his music will develop.

tracks.name: Open My Eyes
tracks.desc: Where to start with future sound collabs.... These are the artists I've looked up to since I found the site and they've all had profound effects on the music I make. Those vocals with that beat and piano, it's so simple and so nice. Too chill.

tracks.name: Monochroma
tracks.desc: More VLTRA... why not. This has been front and center on my ipod for a while because of that bass and that perc, it's all so good. The quintessential VLTRA sounds. Amazing work.

tracks.name: soar
tracks.desc: My fav track by tornsage, one of the first users on here to notice my profile and one of the friendliest people on the site. Those drums at 45 seconds are amazing and remind me of a lot of music I enjoyed in the early 2000's when I was growing up.

tracks.name: innerlude
tracks.desc: So beautiful and really optimistic in a way I like a lot. Amoeba is an artist who has had a profound effect on me with his sick tracks.

tracks.name: M V M M O N
tracks.desc: This is so heavy and that saw synth is too good. Blew my head off when I first heard it. Nominal is a god; EVERYDAY ANYONE is the best track on the site but everyone already knows that one so I want to spread this oldie.

tracks.name: terminator dad
tracks.desc: The bassline in this is just so catchy, listening to this while commuting home is always a pleasure, a personal fav of Vltra's work.

tracks.name: all good things...
tracks.desc: Absolutely breathtaking. The emotion this track brings to me is like no other. Chills me to my core, in a good way.

tracks.name: [Snowflake.]
tracks.desc: Dzool has such a unique style when it comes to his tracks and that's what I love about them. <3

tracks.name: rmx 4 sum1
tracks.desc: This song is perfect to listen to on the way home, late at night.

tracks.name: Visage
tracks.desc: I love the sound design in this track, the build up just before the track is so refreshing

tracks.name: I Still Regret It
tracks.desc: This track melted my mind when I first heard it. The drop at 1:30 is beyond amazing. This track still holds strong as one of my favorites Xavrockbeats has made.

tracks.name: 5 - APARTMENTS
tracks.desc: Without a doubt my favorite out of the ep. The strings in this and the beat just go together so well. I've listened to this track more times than I can count and yet I still love it massively !

tracks.name: Derendorf
tracks.desc: My go-to audiotooler for Techno. But this track in particular grabbed me, I bloody love it!

tracks.name: Démolit le Système à Grands Coups de Kicks
Aphelion ( Mikke and The Seahorse )
tracks.desc: With this track there goes a free ticked for a calm journey through the universe.

tracks.name: Equilibrium
tracks.desc: Super cool Psy = )

tracks.name: CORE (MP Editz)
tracks.desc: I like dirty mics!

tracks.name: limit break
tracks.desc: Awesome, for me one tracks which truly shows what Audiotool makes possible in the right hands.

tracks.name: Layers (Lost myself) WIP
tracks.desc: Deep mindfuck! I love this on afterhours : P

tracks.name: VHS
tracks.desc: This track catapults you some years back in time.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - QVIN
tracks.desc: Below the surface of the deep sea.

tracks.name: DDSS
tracks.desc: Relaxed Beats.

tracks.name: cold summer (will move)
tracks.desc: Amoeba, Not only have I collaborated with him a couple of times but he has always been a big inspiration for my unique style of music.

tracks.name: Stay Fly (ft True)
tracks.desc: This song was very inspirational when I started on my second album Brother Earth. Tornsage has always been a large supporter and fan of my music and in the past has encouraged me to take risks in my music.

tracks.name: ONCE
tracks.desc: This is one of my favourite songs for being so mellow and light.

tracks.name: [Lulu]
tracks.desc: ... is so jazzy and mellow and all of his music still inspires me to make beats.

tracks.name: Elaq
tracks.desc: Just another amazing artist that has always caught my attention.

tracks.name: Silhouette
tracks.desc: I have watched him grow and will always support his music.

tracks.name: Chrono Realm
tracks.desc: My favorite track that I have made on Audiotool is: 12. Ginseng by @superneonsonvegasairwaves

tracks.name: Last Minut35 - Down the street
tracks.desc: My favorite track from one of the nicest people I’ve met on here. I love the melody of the track at 1:43 and something about the song feels motivational. Overall it’s just a great track. I definitely recommend it.

tracks.name: technicolor//mountains (S p o t s & oceanic)
I still think about you all the time. (and it kills me)
tracks.desc: This song is so beautiful and expressive. It’s one of the most emotional songs and you can feel the pain Lewi was going through when you listen to this. I love every five minutes and thirty seconds of this beautiful song. And shoutout to @xeryph for the DL link!

tracks.name: SUMMER NIGHTS
tracks.desc: My summer jam! It can bring a smile to anyone’s face. Tim has mastered Tropical House, and in my opinion this is his best. The vocal chops and piano are perfect. A great song to get up and dance!

tracks.name: Sexy Time with Lux and Kav
tracks.desc: Luxior and Kavil get sexy with this jazzy track! But, being serious this song is nice. The chords are so juicy and the drum patterns are amazing. This song probably doesn’t get as much love as it would if it had a more serious title, so if you haven’t already, give this song a listen. You’ll be satisfied.

tracks.name: Evangeline the Oyster Girl
tracks.desc: This is not what you normally hear from The Seahorse. I love how it is not unhappy. My favorite part of this track is the percussion. You can tell how much work was put into this song. I recommend giving a listen.

tracks.name: You're No Fun
tracks.desc: A classic. I am not a fan of Dubstep, unless it’s this song. SadfaCe hasn’t been active recently, but I really wish he was. Anyways, the bass on this is so crazy, and it has a real catchy melody. I didn’t always like this song but it has definitely grown on me.

tracks.name: Firefly (Ft. Luxior)
tracks.desc: This was the second song I heard after getting on Audiotool and I decided I had to start using the app at this point. At this point in time (when I heard the song), my music taste was basically Firefly styled tracks so yeah, shoutout Xavi for convincing me to use AT.

tracks.name: Already Here
tracks.desc: I love this song so much. It makes me very happy (especially because this is a one-of-a-kind track). Check out his other songs for some great Lo-Fi Hip-Hop.

tracks.name: [VHs.RiP.]
tracks.desc: The melty goodness of the static keeps me coming back for more of Dzool’s music.

tracks.name: Jingle Bells - Synth Barbershop
tracks.desc: I can’t believe someone took the time to find the right formants for a human voice. Truly the mad genius of Audiotool.

tracks.name: Taste of Night
tracks.desc: One of my favorite songs on AT. The vocals paired with the various beautiful synths bring together an image that you can only see when you experience it for yourself. If there’s one song in this list you have to listen to, it’s this one. just kidding, listen to all of them :p

tracks.name: MUGEN
tracks.desc: The synths in this song are so beautiful. cuddlex’s song are and have been super unique and this is no exception.

tracks.name: Azure. (1K Remix Entry)
tracks.desc: I listened to this song for 1.5 hours straight today while I was working out. The trance vibe Synthonix managed to create is insanely hypnotic and the plucks are so yummy.

tracks.name: Still.
tracks.desc: Beautiful production. Kavil’s songs really challenged me to learn good music theory and I really appreciate him for that.

tracks.name: Destiny
tracks.desc: Vulkron was pretty much one of the guys who got me into Audiotool. He became an inspiration and I learned a lot from him. I remember when this track came out and I shit bricks over how I had talked to the guy who made this kind of stuff. <3 Vulkron

tracks.name: Efflorescence [Melodic Dubstep]
tracks.desc: Zerod, basically just god if he came down to earth trying to make melodic Dupstep on Audiotool. Honestly, to this day I cannot understand why he's not signed up to a big lable or making thousands of dollars a day for selling his stuff. He has successfully mastered sampling and flowing drums, combined with some of the best track compositions ever seen on Audiotool.

tracks.name: lose control
tracks.desc: Teq-oh wait no. He changed his name to ViNL and that name is awesome, but no matter the name. He will always be one of the best and most skillful artists on this website. I expect to see him and some big name do a collaboration one day. He has some of the best drum skills on this website in my opinion

tracks.name: ion. [electro]
tracks.desc: Synthonix. This guy basically made Complextro a thing on Audiotool. His chord progressions are really good, I have studied countless of his tracks to see if I could learn a thing or two. And I always could. I bet Synthy could try almost any modern genre and be a god at it.

tracks.name: Sol3r x StageFright - Storm Chasers
tracks.desc: Stagefright is really talented, even though this is one of his earlier works, it instantly gained a lot of love from me and the Audiotool community. The drop is so smooth and really gives off the vibe that I'm running from a tornado or a huge storm. I encourage you to check out Stagefrights stuff when you get the chance!

tracks.name: Analog Tears
tracks.desc: Now its Xav's time to show us what he's got, and it wouldn't be hard to tell with this track as a reference! The chords in Analog Tears are so filling and every time I hear the voice in the middle of the track I get goose bumps on the back neck. Simply amazing

tracks.name: EARTHBOUND (Tim Derry)
tracks.desc: Tim Derry! Where do I start, tropical king of AT? Musical god. Or maybe even one of the most friendly guys on Audiotool. The guitars in this track are really warm and comfy and it's one of the best entries to a remix competition I've seen in a long time. Tim Derry has helped me on a few tracks and I've watch him produce. It's god damn amazing how efficient he is, which only adds to his talent.

tracks.name: sinister
tracks.desc: Of course Luxior had to be on here. Sinister is so amazingly produced it's actually scary. To this day I cannot find much that he did wrong on this. Melody is banging and the sound design is on point. I keep finding find myself listening to this.

tracks.name: Werbs - The Final Battle (ExPe Entry)
tracks.desc: I love this track! And I love the artist as well. Expe/Ethan has been a huge inspiration for me on Audiotool. He was one of the first people I listened to and I think this track shows both his skills as an artist and how to modify an existing setup. #ExpeIsLove

tracks.name: Rue Jue - Into The Sky Blue (Kepz Remix)
Don't throw rainbows when you live in a glass house
Duty is as Heavy as a Mountain, Death is as Light as a Feather
Finding the words to say to you
